headline: CONTANGO OUTLINES $63M EXPLORATION PUSH: 18,000m at Lucky Shot Alaska, $25M Infill at BC's Kitsault Valley
snippet: Contango Silver & Gold unveiled its largest-ever $63M 2026 exploration program, headlined by 18,000m of drilling at the Lucky Shot gold project 112 miles north of Anchorage and a $25M, 40,000m infill campaign at Kitsault Valley in northern British Columbia — one of Canada's most advanced critical metals corridors.

headline: NEVGOLD HITS 99% GOLD RECOVERY and 1.11% Antimony Over 6.1m — Upsizes Financing to C$42.2M in Three Weeks
snippet: NevGold Corp. confirmed 99% gold recovery in phase II metallurgical testing and intercepted 1.11% antimony over 6.1 metres at Limousine Butte in Nevada, while upsizing its private placement to C$42.2M — positioning the company for a Q2 2026 maiden antimony-gold MRE and potential near-term production by 2027 as Western antimony supply chains scramble to diversify away from China.

headline: MORGAN STANLEY SLASHES Gold Target Nearly 10% to $5,200 — Six-Week Selloff 'Completely Changes the Tone'
snippet: Morgan Stanley cut its H2 2026 gold price target from $5,700 to $5,200/oz after gold shed roughly 8% from all-time highs to near $4,700/oz, citing rising real yields and delayed Fed rate-cut expectations — with the bank now forecasting the first rate cut no earlier than September or December 2026.

headline: ELORO'S ISKA ISKA GROWS TO 1.15 BILLION OZ Silver Equivalent — Indicated Grade Up 65% vs 2023 MRE
snippet: Eloro Resources' updated MRE at Iska Iska in Bolivia now totals 1.15 billion ounces silver equivalent across indicated and inferred categories, with 85.17 Mt grading 40 g/t Ag in the Indicated category alone — a 65% grade improvement over the 2023 initial estimate — alongside tin, zinc, lead, and gold credits at what may become one of the world's largest silver-polymetallic deposits.

headline: HIGH-GRADE SILVER HIT: Silver Acadia Returns 7.1m at 396.8 g/t Ag and 2.38 g/t Au at New Brunswick's Bathurst Camp
snippet: Silver Acadia's Phase 1 drill program at the Nicholas-Denys project in the historic Bathurst Mining Camp returned 7.1m at 396.8 g/t silver and 2.38 g/t gold in its best hole, confirming multi-kilometre high-grade silver-gold potential across a district the market still prices as a zinc play.

headline: MIRASOL DRILLS 7KM FROM FILO DEL SOL: Sobek Confirms Copper-Gold Hydrothermal System in Chile's Vicuña District
snippet: Mirasol Resources confirmed a structurally controlled hydrothermal system with anomalous copper-gold geochemistry at its Sobek project — 7km west of Filo del Sol and 3km from NGEx's Lunahuasi discovery in Chile's Vicuña District — with assays from a second, deeper drill hole pending and geological modeling ongoing.

headline: SILVER PRICES ROSE EVEN AS DEMAND SLIPPED — Years of Physical Squeeze Finally Forced Buyers to Bid Higher
snippet: Money Metals' Mike Maharrey argues that silver's 147% intrayear gain in 2025 — from $28.84 to $71.30 — defied conventional demand-supply logic because years of physical market tightening built beneath the surface before erupting: holders simply refused to sell until buyers bid high enough, a dynamic that remains in place as the sixth consecutive annual deficit unfolds.

headline: GOLD SIDEWAYS, NERVES HIGH: Precious Metals Trade Flat as US-Iran Stalemate Blocks Strait of Hormuz
snippet: Gold plateaued near $4,700–$4,750/oz this week as the diplomatic standstill between the US and Iran keeps the Strait of Hormuz under pressure, elevating oil prices and inflation expectations while strong US economic data — falling jobless claims, rising retail sales — dampens safe-haven appetite and delays any Fed pivot.

headline: AFRICA DEVELOPERS STEP UP: Aging Mine Base and No Major Replacements Create a Generational Supply Gap
snippet: With senior gold mines exhausting reserves and mine-site drilling now consuming a record 45% of all exploration capital globally, a cohort of well-funded African gold developers is positioning to fill the coming supply gap — potentially offering early-stage exposure to the next tier of world-class deposits at historically elevated gold prices above $4,700/oz.

headline: UGANDA JOINS THE GOLD RUSH: Central Bank Launches Domestic Buying Programme, Targets 7–10 Tonnes Annually
snippet: The Bank of Uganda launched a three-year pilot Domestic Gold Purchase Programme — contracting EuroGold Refinery and Feldstein Trading to supply a minimum 100kg of refined gold in Q2 2026 and scaling to 7–10 tonnes annually — part of a broader African central bank pivot toward gold as a reserve asset amid dollar-denominated risk.
